doc.title: Short-Term Lease Election
doc.event: Pryor Co. signs a 9-month lease on storage space at $2,000 per month and elects the short-term lease exception. Prepare Pryor's monthly entry.

lines.why: Short-term election: expense the payments straight-line, period.
lines.trap: No ROU asset, no lease liability — that's the entire point of the election.

memorize.body: Short-term election: lease term ≤ 12 months AND no purchase option the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise.
It's an ELECTION, not a requirement — and it evaporates if the term stretches past 12 months.

questions.prompt: Under the short-term election, what lease liability does Pryor record at commencement?

distractors.feedback: You capitalized the gross payments (9 × 2,000). The short-term election skips the balance sheet entirely — no liability at all.
2,000 is the monthly expense/payment. The election means NO liability is set up — the answer is zero.

build.scaffold: Simplest entry in the chapter — two lines. If you reached for ROU, re-read the election.
